headline: JPMorgan Hires Goldman’s Zhang to Co-Head China IB Business
summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. hired Zhang Yi to co-head its China investment banking business with Michelle Wang, according to an internal memo. Zhang comes from Goldman Sachs Group Inc. which is losing its second senior China banker in the past two months.

content.title: JPMorgan Hires Goldman’s Zhang to Co-Head China IB Business
content.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. hired Zhang Yi to co-head its China investment banking business with Michelle Wang, according to an internal memo. Zhang comes from Goldman Sachs Group Inc. which is losing its second senior China banker in the past two months.

headline: SEC Proposal to Eliminate Quarterly Earnings Is Being Readied. What It Gets Wrong—and Right.
summary: The Securities and Exchange Commission, in an attempt to address a long-simmering disconnect between long-term investors and Wall Street when it comes to corporate reporting, appears to be focusing on the wheat of quarterly corporate reporting.  It may do better to address the chaff of quarterly earnings guidance.  At the behest of President Donald Trump, and a groundswell of smaller company advocates, the SEC is looking to end a longstanding requirement that listed companies publish detailed investor updates every three months.

content.title: SEC Proposal to Eliminate Quarterly Earnings Is Being Readied. What It Gets Wrong—and Right.
content.summary: The Securities and Exchange Commission, in an attempt to address a long-simmering disconnect between long-term investors and Wall Street when it comes to corporate reporting, appears to be focusing on the wheat of quarterly corporate reporting.  It may do better to address the chaff of quarterly earnings guidance.  At the behest of President Donald Trump, and a groundswell of smaller company advocates, the SEC is looking to end a longstanding requirement that listed companies publish detailed investor updates every three months.

headline: 1 S&P 500 Stock to Own for Decades and 2 We Question

headline: JPMorgan sued over $328M crypto ‘Ponzi’ scheme
summary: Despite red flags, Chase “turned a blind eye and continued servicing the accounts used to perpetrate the fraud, earning substantial fees from the hundreds of millions of dollars” it touched in a Florida-based crypto scheme.

content.title: JPMorgan sued over $328M crypto ‘Ponzi’ scheme
content.summary: Despite red flags, Chase “turned a blind eye and continued servicing the accounts used to perpetrate the fraud, earning substantial fees from the hundreds of millions of dollars” it touched in a Florida-based crypto scheme.

headline: JPMorgan Chase (JPM) Valuation Check As Lending To AI Exposed Private Credit Tightens
summary: JPMorgan Chase (JPM) has tightened lending to private credit funds, marking down some loans tied to software companies seen as vulnerable to AI related disruption, a move that has stirred investor debate around risk appetite. headline: Treasury Yields Tick Lower in Cautious Market
summary: Treasury yields are down slightly. U.S. government borrowing costs have risen decisively since the start of the Iran conflict as higher energy costs threaten inflation. But the trajectory for shorter-term yields especially may stabilize, bounded by dueling risks, JPMorgan's Jay Barry writes.

content.title: Treasury Yields Tick Lower in Cautious Market
content.summary: Treasury yields are down slightly. U.S. government borrowing costs have risen decisively since the start of the Iran conflict as higher energy costs threaten inflation. But the trajectory for shorter-term yields especially may stabilize, bounded by dueling risks, JPMorgan's Jay Barry writes.

headline: SoFi Technologies Slides 4% as JPMorgan Lending Restrictions Rattle the Private Credit Market
summary: SoFi Technologies (NASDAQ:SOFI) stock dropped 4% Tuesday, with shares sliding below $17 as of midday. The move extends a rough stretch for the stock, which has shed 37% year to date even as the underlying business keeps hitting records. The catalyst today has nothing to do with SoFi’s own numbers. headline: How The Wise (LSE:WISE) Narrative Is Shifting As Analysts Recalibrate Targets And Expectations
summary: Wise’s fair value price target has been trimmed to £11.62 from £11.75, while JPMorgan has lowered its target to £12.25 from £13.85, bringing projections closer to where the Street now sits. These shifts come alongside an Overweight rating at JPMorgan and an upgrade from another major broker, which together indicate a more balanced mix of confidence and caution around Wise. As you read on, you will see how to track this evolving narrative and what these moves might mean for your own...

headline: JPMorganChase Declares Common Stock Dividend
summary: NEW YORK, March 17, 2026--JPMorgan Chase & Co. (NYSE: JPM) ("JPMorganChase" or the "Firm") declared a quarterly dividend on the outstanding shares of the common stock of JPMorganChase. Information can be found on the Firm’s Investor Relations website at https://www.jpmorganchase.com/ir/news.

content.title: JPMorganChase Declares Common Stock Dividend
content.summary: NEW YORK, March 17, 2026--JPMorgan Chase & Co. (NYSE: JPM) ("JPMorganChase" or the "Firm") declared a quarterly dividend on the outstanding shares of the common stock of JPMorganChase. Information can be found on the Firm’s Investor Relations website at https://www.jpmorganchase.com/ir/news.

headline: JPMorganChase to Host First-Quarter 2026 Earnings Call
summary: NEW YORK, March 17, 2026--As previously announced, JPMorgan Chase & Co. (NYSE: JPM) ("JPMorganChase" or the "Firm") will host a conference call to review first-quarter 2026 financial results on Tuesday, April 14, 2026 at 8:30 a.m. (ET). The results are scheduled to be released at approximately 7:00 a.m. (ET). The live audio webcast and presentation slides will be available on www.jpmorganchase.com under Investor Relations, Events & Presentations.

content.title: JPMorganChase to Host First-Quarter 2026 Earnings Call
content.summary: NEW YORK, March 17, 2026--As previously announced, JPMorgan Chase & Co. (NYSE: JPM) ("JPMorganChase" or the "Firm") will host a conference call to review first-quarter 2026 financial results on Tuesday, April 14, 2026 at 8:30 a.m. (ET). The results are scheduled to be released at approximately 7:00 a.m. (ET). The live audio webcast and presentation slides will be available on www.jpmorganchase.com under Investor Relations, Events & Presentations.

headline: JPMorgan to Add 300 Jobs, Anchor Boston's South Station Tower
summary: Bank consolidates offices into 250,000-square-foot space starting 2028, expanding workforce and targeting growth across banking and advisory segments.

headline: Treasury Yields Fall Slightly
summary: An auction of new 20-year bonds was met with “solid” demand, with bidding from non-dealers slightly above the recent average, per Vail Hartman of BMO Capital Markets.  0858 ET – U.S. Treasury yields are down slightly.  “With labor market concerns still lingering, and the potential for sustained higher oil prices keeping the Fed cautious, we see a path for near-term stabilization in front-end yields,” Barry writes.

content.title: Treasury Yields Fall Slightly
content.summary: An auction of new 20-year bonds was met with “solid” demand, with bidding from non-dealers slightly above the recent average, per Vail Hartman of BMO Capital Markets.  0858 ET – U.S. Treasury yields are down slightly.  “With labor market concerns still lingering, and the potential for sustained higher oil prices keeping the Fed cautious, we see a path for near-term stabilization in front-end yields,” Barry writes.
